payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, so let's actually start this game, guys. Thank you for being patient with our intro here. We've been gone for two weeks. We have a lot to talk about, so thank you for letting us get that out. So picking up where we left off, the party completed the venture of taking the horn of Orcus to a place of safety and sealing it away, hopefully for eternity, with the help of Lady Kima, to the scalebearers. After heading to the basement of the Platinum Sanctuary in the city of Vasselheim, they've succeeded to do so. Vasselheim, which is a distant city to the far northwest of the continent they're used to. This city is a very devout, religion-based city, and currently considered the oldest bastion of civilization in the known civilized world. After taking a skyship across the ocean to get there, sealing the Horn, you guys also discover upon doing so that the ruins, long-forgotten ruins, of what appears to be an ancient temple to Sarenrae were recently discovered. While Sarenrae does not have a strong following in this city, the few that are trying to raise it ask for Pike's help to stay back and help build it and bring it to its former glory. Hopefully to re-establish a basis of worship in this city, and she decided to help these folks. The party then steps away from the ruins of the tower, sad but understanding of the circumstance.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Is he on his way?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's on his way, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well when he arrives... In the meantime--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Where are you going?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to get something to drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's getting a drink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As Tiberius has an aneurysm and falls to the ground--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes! I go over and provide very vigorous CPR. No mouth to mouth, just that hand part.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. All right, make a death save.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: What? Ow! (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: And I brought Scanlan with me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Like a trailing fart.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughing) "Like a trailing fart."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: That couldn't have worked out better.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh really?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What is he wearing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: "Asp Hole?" What does it say? "Where you" what?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's the Asp Hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Asp. Hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just a bar that I like to go to. Down at the Asp Hole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I got shit for talking about bears today.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm pretty sure that's somewhere in Emon. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And we'll find it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we'll find the Asp Hole.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Critical Role all of a sudden becomes the greatest grass-watching game of all time. As you all stand there, you have to decide your next course of action. You stand in the muddy road, taking in the cold dusk air as the sun slowly sets to the west. Now, this area of the city has a vastly different feel than the Platinum Sanctuary. In the haste to make it to the ruin, you've passed out of the section that seems to be under Bahamut's watch and into a different area. In this area, most of the buildings are reinforced with ruddy iron plates. Plumes of smoke rise up from a handful of heavy smokestacks. You can see over the rooftops in the distance toward the center of this section what looks like a massive dark red statue of a muscular bearded man.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Perhaps we could sit on a nearby rooftop and watch Pike work.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Just for the next eight months?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'd be all right with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's not creepy at all.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys begin your approach, even in the darkness, you can see the figure, this human-like man. Barrel-chested. Massive muscles. What looks almost like iron bracers around his wrists. He is carved or sculpted from solid iron or plates that have been put together from some sort of iron initial structure. The beard is long and goes to about mid-chest. He carries in one hand this long, thick shortsword and has furs and leathers about his legs and midsection. You recognize this to be a very hefty depiction of the deity Kord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I got this.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Boom!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: That's so cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Natural four.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Because we know our shit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "This late in the evening--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Be a pity not to have one more.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You wish to fight?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wish? It's more like an itch. A need.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He puts his arm out and the crowd steps back a bit and he takes a good, long head-to-toe up-and-down look at you, walks around from side to side inspecting you. He gets a little bit close and reaches out, not even asking, he starts grabbing the side of your arms.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I start growling a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, watch out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Calm down, kitty, calm down." Steps back. "Well, it's not too late. What caliber of fighter do you consider yourself?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Caliber?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Who do you want to fight?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who are the options?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Could he fight anyone?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is my friend, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm more of his manager than his friend, actually. Hi, I'm Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Pleasure."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Pleasure's all mine. My brute over here, who can he battle, what are his options?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, first and foremost, all bets begin at 100 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Caliber?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Who do you want to fight?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Who are the options?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Could he fight anyone?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is my friend, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm more of his manager than his friend, actually. Hi, I'm Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Pleasure."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Pleasure's all mine. My brute over here, who can he battle, what are his options?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "This late in the evening--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll fight him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I said I'll fight him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I apologize. If you're going to have someone enter the arena worth betting on, they have to go up against one of our proven fighters first. However, once your friend here gets his face beaten in, you're more than welcome to join him in the ring thereafter."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I haven't laughed so hard in my entire life. Face beaten, he says. Face beaten!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You're mighty confident, aren't you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's my middle-- I only have one name. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: If I did.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It would be that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Go ahead and roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I took a drink from the cask of ale--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, I come over to Grog and I just look in his eyes and I say, listen, you've got this, I can't give you anything else, but you've got this, man. And I also very quickly take out my Potion of Fire Giant Strength and I slip it to him before he takes a drink of his ale. I don't know if I hand it to him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (squeaks) Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What did you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's a six. So eight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. As you walk towards him and begin looking in his eyes, as you begin to pull out the flask, a hand grasps your wrist. And you glance over and you can see one of the burly human gentlemen that escorted Kern the Hammer in is currently grasping your arm and says, "I'm watching you. One more try and I'll throw you in there. We'll go down ourselves."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's for my asthma. I promise.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Shoves you back away, still clutching it. Looks to you, Grog, up into your eyes. "You going to climb down and fight or you going to be a pussy about it?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's a really good question. I would like to rage. And I jump in the fucking pit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You leap over the side, completely avoiding the ladder, both feet hitting the ground with a heavy thud sound. The packed dust kicks up around your feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Come on, Phillip! Kick his ass!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At which point, the other orc's finished cracking his knuckles, just kind of wipes the spit off the edge of his chin, gives you a look (growls) and you see a bit of foam toss from the side of his mouth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Nobody fucks with Phil.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can smell you from here, Kern!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, so both the first two hit. As he comes low for a full-on uppercut, hitting you for, that would be six points of damage (whack) under the chin. As you're currently reeling from the impact, he crouches down and is going to go for a knee to the middle of your groin. That is another six points of damage (whack) right in the Phillip junk. You instinctively give out a (grunts) from the impact. After that point, he comes up and it looks like he tries to go for some sort of a pile-drive grapple, and you instinctively kick off of him and his third attack is completely wasted. It's now your go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, so six damage. All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And then four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is what I use? For my fists? I got big-ass fists! I got tattoos and shit on them. I never shower, they fucking stink!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You could do more damage if you hit him with the die, because it's sharp, so you'd actually get more damage with the d4.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So your modifier is plus-- it's plus three for the modifier?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, my modifier's plus four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, and then what did you roll on that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Two. Minus two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Two, then five total.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, five, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go. All righty, cool. So you hit him across the face and you hit him to the other side. He comes back, aligns his chin, and you can see he's now stooping in for some sort of an uppercut as he slips to the side a little bit with a sidestep. That will be a 25. A 20. And a one. So what's your AC?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Go ahead and roll an attack. You have advantage on this currently, because he can't see it coming.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Riki-oh style. I like it. All right. So. As you go ahead, go ahead and just roll a basic attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck. Thank you, god. 26.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 26. Make a dexterity save on his end-- no, that won't work. So you throw the dirt up. As he's rushing towards you to follow up on the attack, the dirt goes streaming into his eyes and for a second he stumbles and starts trying to wipe his face. As he does, he has the blood on his hands that's also getting in his eyes, the blood's starting to cake up a bit, and he's just reaching out in the area. So that's your first attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Because I feel like we have a kinship, I take my foot and I throw it right up into his grundle, just soccer-kick the shit out of his nuts. (laughter)

payload.raw_text: You can see, that blow should have taken him out, because there was nothing left.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: As a half-orc, does he got those big teeth?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: There are little tusks there, yeah.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: They're small to pull. You slam down on his face. (crunch) You can see the jaw crack open. The tongue instinctively moves around a little, but the jaw is definitely broken from the impact.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's like a dance! It's like a beautiful dance.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I would say given the circumstances, it's nice that he's trying, but it's been a while since you've been bested in one-on-one combat, and a part of you is deeply hurt by it. But at the same time, there is that burning fire, that need to come back and vengefully tear him down from his precipice when you're ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That was painful.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That was so close.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I go down and nurse him, nurse his head, hold him, and just tell him, Grog, you won! You did it, buddy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception roll.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Grog, as you make your way into the tavern, there is, though in your solemn state you can see a number of the patrons who were watching from the inside who were part of the crowd all give a round of applause to you as you enter. The barkeep who turns to you, a human fellow who's missing one eye, has a tufted, spotty beard and happily extends a large tankard of ale and says, "On the house for you, friend! You fought well tonight."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll need to earn our money back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is that, yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Or you could just give it to us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I walk away.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is there a whorehouse here in Vasselheim?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You're asking this question?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A curious laugh comes across the bookie's face and he says, "The third floor of the Bellows'--" What did I call it, again?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The Bellows' Respite.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Bellows' Respite. Sorry, I was in the moment. "The third floor of the Bellows' Respite does itself run as a brothel, if that's what you're inquiring."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That is what I was inquiring.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Come on, Grog, we'll get you ice cream.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll get you ice cream, Grog, and a whore. How's that sound?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Coldstone?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Grog, you can eat the ice cream from the whore.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tiberius, really?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm trying to be creative here. Make him feel good. Fireworks. Prestidigitation.

payload.raw_text: Welcome back, guys. All right, so we had a little break here. Remember to follow our awesome artists on the Twitter. So, let's go ahead and get back to the game so we can-- we have a lot to do tonight. All right. As morning comes to, the rest of you wake up, the first thought, of course, being the heartache for your friend Grog, who is not in any of the rooms.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Never entirely welcome in the company of elves or men, Vax'ildan learned at a young age to skip past formality, preferring instead to invite himself in your door. Along with twin sister Vex'ahlia, Vax was born by a chance encounter between elven royalty and human peasantry. Raised by their mother in their early years, the twins were eventually sent off to their father in the elven capital of Syngorn. But their cool reception among the elves--

payload.raw_text: Yeah, more the money probably for Vex-- but as you wake up, Grog is nowhere to be found in your rooms, of course. He wandered off to presumably the third floor.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Welcome back, guys. All right, so we had a little break here. Remember to follow our awesome artists on the Twitter. So, let's go ahead and get back to the game so we can-- we have a lot to do tonight. All right. As morning comes to, the rest of you wake up, the first thought, of course, being the heartache for your friend Grog, who is not in any of the rooms.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: The heartache for our lost money.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, more the money probably for Vex-- but as you wake up, Grog is nowhere to be found in your rooms, of course. He wandered off to presumably the third floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back, guys. All right, so we had a little break here. Remember to follow our awesome artists on the Twitter. So, let's go ahead and get back to the game so we can-- we have a lot to do tonight. All right. As morning comes to, the rest of you wake up, the first thought, of course, being the heartache for your friend Grog, who is not in any of the rooms.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The heartache for our lost money.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Meals are ordered, you guys are fed as per your purchase of the evening's inn stay. Your meal's still covered by the tavern. Eventually, you find yourselves full and about, I'll say roughly ten, 11 in the morning it would be.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your tankard goes flying out of your hand, on the ground, it spills across the front--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I make my way down to the thing and I tell the bartender, give this warrior water. This might be your problem, Grog. You lost for a reason, and you're a warrior.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, but he's named after beer. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I know that, Vax. I'm just saying.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And you are what you eat.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I cast Invisibility.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Tiberius vanishes. The barkeep hands over a big mug of clear water and sets it in front of Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, this is the very special beer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It's the water that was requested by your friend. Who disappeared."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Water?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It'll get you super drunk.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you feel it? You can feel it, right? You can totally feel it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's pretty smooth. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Keep it coming.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You've been walking away for a while, and you get about roughly ten minutes away before you hear this impact and these horns going off in the distance.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Perhaps shopping is not the best path.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We did just do a lot of shopping.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's go adventuring.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We haven't had an awkward parental confrontation in what feels like months.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys, I'm so nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You really are?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, it's a big thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Have you seen them, ever?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The first time I left home was when I met up with you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (impact) You guys suddenly hear this slamming sound that echoes throughout the nearby city. You hear this impact, like this distant crashing of stone against stone. A few seconds later, you hear this low (horn sound), this horn that's just bellowing out, this deep echoing horn. The rest of the tavern looks around, confused. A couple of individuals step outside to look.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm at the dock, right?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "We've had this blasted thing seen on and off again for the past few weeks. Maybe it's hungry or it's bored, but either way, this multi-headed thing, this hydra-like creature keeps coming in and I don't know, I think it's testing the wall."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Go ask one of the Bastion guys what it was.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we fly up next to one of the shooter guys and say, what was it? What is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you glide up to it, and as you look down, you can see the two Bastions that fell. They're currently being tended to. One of them looks unconscious and rough from the impact of the fall, the other one's walking with a limp, trying to find its way back towards the interior of the wall that leads up to the top of his post. The one you come by has sweat beading down his face and you see he's loaded another bolt and he's looking out, wide-eyed, towards the forest. He goes, "It came back."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What came back?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Following its path, you see that there are tracks going both ways, meaning the creature as it came through towards the city, it turned its path about and then exited the same pathway. So as you're following it along for a good 15 minutes or so, you come across a small opening in the forest where you can see what looks to be a very messily partially devoured carcass of something.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just like the old days.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Now we've touched down and we're traveling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are in the forest pushing through various walkways. Go ahead and make a survival check with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Survival check? Is that for her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. She's tracking the creature.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, natural 20! (cheering)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, as big as some of the umber hulks you've fought. But it looks humanoid-ish, more primate-like. The more you look at it, it looks like it may have been at one point a large, massive ape of some kind. But it has been torn open, limbs scattered all across this small clearing. You can see there are a series of bite and scratch marks across it, and most of its internal organs are missing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, natural 20! (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Following its path, you see that there are tracks going both ways, meaning the creature as it came through towards the city, it turned its path about and then exited the same pathway. So as you're following it along for a good 15 minutes or so, you come across a small opening in the forest where you can see what looks to be a very messily partially devoured carcass of something.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is it big?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It looks like it was pretty big, it's definitely spread out over this area.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think it ate something here, you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Good ranging.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to try and identify the animal that it ate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 13? You look at it, it appears to be some sort of furred beast. Huge in size at one point, it is long-dead for at least--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Like moose-big or bigger?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. You continue moving forward off the track for another ten minutes or so before you see some movement ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Is it plant life?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Plant life, tree roots, heavy thick branches that have fallen or have grown out and interwoven. Occasional rocks or natural formations that are jutting out of the earth.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's continue on as fast as possible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make another tracking check, with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man, another one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to inspire her with a little song of inspiration. (singing) And the ranger's going to range, range, range, range, range. And the tracker's going to track, track, track, track, track. I'm just going to watch, watch, watch, watch, watch. Watch her range.

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Watch her range.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do I need to add?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A d10.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh good. Three points.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You all get a free round, and then we roll initiative.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, I used it at the fight that you didn't want to fight in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what's the DC?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17. All right, it does make its save, so half damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: How do I count these? There's a bunch of sevens. Stupid ones.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: All together?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, that's 28.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 43. Awesome. So a total of 22 points of damage from a save. So as it's stepping into the water, it takes an additional, I'll say, additional 2d8 from being in the water. I'll roll them here. Additional nine damage to that. As it pulls back, the stone around it, the rogue leaps up and slams it with a blade, its heads turn around, and as it does, you turn around, vanish from sight, and then... you cast Greater Invisibility or just Invisibility?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Greater.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Release your hands and a torrent of blue electrical energy rockets forth from your hands, streaking out, some parts leaving arcs that hit the nearby forests and then slamming into its body, its exposed heads. You can see the arcs now shooting through between the various necks and bodies. It writhes and streaks about, slamming its body into part of the stone that cracks and heaves from the impact, still holding it in place. Well done. You want to move, or is that your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm going, what are the big lumps, are those trees?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: These are trees. Anywhere walking on the trunk around it is difficult terrain, you cannot pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm just going to go right behind that tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That ends your turn. Who else wants to do anything this round?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to shoot him.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, 29. You turn around the corner, the energy still crackling around it from the Chain Lightning. You unleash your own Lightning Bolt that hits it from the side, slamming into the side of one of the heads. The head actually pops from the impact. It's now got four heads.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Athletics.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Because there's no branches nearby, you have to--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Six! I'm going to stay on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You slide back to the trunk, turn around and grab your bow and prepare yourself. Trinket, anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket's going to stay right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll move and do Lightning. Why not? Just try to get as much as possible. Just so I won't hit whoever that is.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That would probably be right there. All right, so you unleash a Lightning Bolt?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll damage, 8d6. Does not make its save. Rolled a five.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: By the way, we need seven more subs, which is ironically the same number of heads the hydra has.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Seven more subs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Seven more subs?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're going to get a bear!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, gotcha. That was the total 8d6?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That was 6d6.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 8d6 for Lightning Bolt. Roll two more.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten more!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 17 points of damage as you hack once again, slamming into the side of one of the necks, causing a gout of purplish blood to spray over the right side of your body. That ends the combat round for the surprise round. The creature is now aware that you're definitely there. Everyone roll initiative. All right, so 25 to 20?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Right now, you can't see because there's a tree in the way, but you're Pepperbox distance from here. You could try Bad News, but you'd have to set up in place.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm going to start curling around the tree I'm near. I'm going to take some movement to fly over the pond back here. And while I'm making my little round, I'm going to take two shots with the Pepperbox.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so as the carpet's gliding past, you're basically strafing it and (gunshots). Go ahead and fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You have a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She has a bear? We got a bear!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, there's going to be a giant Trinket. That's so stupid.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Full-on misfire. (cackles) Yeah, I misfire. I don't care; we got a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. We got a bear. As you strafe past, you pull your Pepperbox and go to fire. Suddenly there's a dull sound, and as you look at your gun, it explodes for a second in your face. You barely manage to get your visage out of the way as the barrel itself is heated up and you have to shake it off. Yeah, your Pepperbox is looking a bit rough right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's fine; I'm putting it away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So everyone else has gone but Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Me? Can I run all the way up and try and pull a Vax, like he did?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, I want to try that! And I rage as I attack it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, as you rush up, axe in your hand, you rush up the side of the stone ramp that she's created and it's right in slamming distance. Go ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I swing away. 24.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and the second. 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16, no. 16 misses. And then?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's it, just the two. I'm not frenzied. Yes. 17.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: For initiative, yeah. 15 to ten?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so as the carpet's gliding past, you're basically strafing it and (gunshots). Go ahead and fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You have a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She has a bear? We got a bear!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, there's going to be a giant Trinket. That's so stupid.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Full-on misfire. (cackles) Yeah, I misfire. I don't care; we got a bear.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. We got a bear. As you strafe past, you pull your Pepperbox and go to fire. Suddenly there's a dull sound, and as you look at your gun, it explodes for a second in your face. You barely manage to get your visage out of the way as the barrel itself is heated up and you have to shake it off. Yeah, your Pepperbox is looking a bit rough right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's fine; I'm putting it away.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. So everyone else has gone but Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Me? Can I run all the way up and try and pull a Vax, like he did?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, I want to try that! And I rage as I attack it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, as you rush up, axe in your hand, you rush up the side of the stone ramp that she's created and it's right in slamming distance. Go ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I swing away. 24.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and the second. 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16, no. 16 misses. And then?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's it, just the two. I'm not frenzied. Yes. 17.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 17 points of damage as you hack once again, slamming into the side of one of the necks, causing a gout of purplish blood to spray over the right side of your body. That ends the combat round for the surprise round. The creature is now aware that you're definitely there. Everyone roll initiative. All right, so 25 to 20?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: 16. First good roll I've ever had.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. It is now the hydra's turn. It rolled a really high initiative. The two heads are going to untangle themselves. That's their attack. Now it only has four strikes this turn, two of which are going to be against you and two of which are going to be against you. All right, so. Two of the heads rear back, lunging at Vax over here. That's going to be a 29. Second one is going to be a 19.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so it rolled a 17 on its second save. The first one, it did not resist because it rolled a three. So what you do as you cast Telekinesis once, you use the sorcery points to cast it again. You can't get all the heads, but you can grab two of them, one focus for each telekinetic thing. You take both heads and (whack, whack) slam them three times into each other and then twist the necks around at the point where both of them are biting onto their own necks after they've torn into a second. For that, they're both going to take 2d8 impact damage. That's nine plus five. 14 points of impact damage to each head. Both of those heads are currently grappling each other, so they will not be able to attack on its turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: And then I go back against the tree. That is my turn.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, you aren't at a short rest yet. Because right now, as you guys are just standing there, the rest of the other party is now stepping up to you. The main human you now see in armor. He has dirty blond short hair, little bit of stubble on the chin, very well-armored, and the armor itself looks like it's taken a beating. He steps up and says, "All right. I'm going to ask you a question. What the fuck are you doing?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Who can heal really a lot really fast?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket! I get up and run over to Trinket and hold his little head in my hands.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who's bad?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Trinket's unconscious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I run up to Trinket and I cast Cure Wounds on Trinket in a 3rd-level spell slot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Help him!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He's going to be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys rush up--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Vax is rolling down the corpse of the hydra to stand in front of these strangers.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've rushed to Trinket's side, too, from where I'm at.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You're right at Trinket's side. You're flying in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I get closer and do the Superbowl shuffle in front of our new visitors, like, yeah, that's what we did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Trinket heals for 21 damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Trinket gets up, charred but okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Who else is bad?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Trinket looks over at Tiberius and gives you a glare.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I use it again for Vax. Ooh, that's better.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I rolled two hit dice--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It takes about half an hour or so to reap the bits and pieces, then again making your way back, after you've pushed the remainder of the body into the pool itself, letting it slowly sink to the bottom. You all make your way back the path you came in an awkward, quiet march.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Certification is key.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Come on. Gather up the creature's bits." And they all go to start carving into the body of the hydra.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, can we help?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Now I start doing my thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've got the naughty bits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I need to roll for this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't have to, actually. The group are very well trained, and as you go to try to, the tiefling puts her hand down and starts tearing pieces out. There's a dominance, a standoff here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I have a scale? Just one more scale?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want some stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's all right. We'll get it and we'll figure it out when we get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There seems to be a general feel here that you've already broken the law enough. If you really want to fight for it, you can.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's quit while we're ahead.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's deal with the naughty bits later.

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way to what
payload.raw_text: Eventually make your way back to town, skirt around the outer wall to one of the three main gates, this one right on the outskirts of the Braving (stutters). Too many creatures. Braving Grounds. The gates open. You're all led in, first question at the sight of you guys, but Aldor raises his hand and says, "No, they're with us; it's fine." You all walk in, leads you to the outskirts of the Braving Grounds to a new area of the town you have yet to travel to. At which point, he introduces, "Welcome to the Quad Roads. This is where the Slayer's Take makes its place. This is also where the Erathis's Crown is. Best you pay an homage to the deity of civilization while here." He pulls out a small gem around his neck with a small symbol on it, kisses it, and puts it back in his shirt. You guys continue walking to what is now, you can see these large roads that streak across. You've seen them join with the other sections. This is the most central of all the various districts of the city of Vasselheim. There is a very large trade bazaar structure not that far out of the main gate that leads to the center of this district. You can see barkers shouting out left and right to you, various phrases dealing with food and various crops, and creature fresh meat. "Fresh meat out of the Abundance! Takers of the Abundance!" And people yelling out for general goods, packs, leathers, large carts being wheeled past you, until eventually you make your way to what looks like a very old, dark-wood lodge, about three stories high, decorated with dozens of tusks and claws and other trophies that look as old as the building itself. A carved wooden sign stands bolted to the top of the porch that says "Slayer's Take Guildhall" in the common tongue. Approaching the door, still that awkward quiet keeping over the group, there is an engraving into the doorway-- it's pushed into the wood-- that reads, "Here is where the hunters gather, keen eyes and blades at ready. We cease the unknown's cackling laughter. Our will is strong, our members plenty. We stand against the tide of darkness. We do the deeds few men can handle. Reap forth a trophy from the carcass. We are your strength, we are your candle." Aldor does a knock on the door. The door opens, an eye looks through, and the door swiftly opens as another individual who's dressed in a makeshift leather and hide armor steps aside. You're all let in, carrying the large hydra heads through that have been gathering looks from the individuals watching you charge through the streets. To the center of the room, you see what looks to be amongst the loose cast-iron candelabras that are affixed to the rafters, small candles flicker for the interior light, you see a long desk where a halfling male of muscular build sits eagerly rubbing his hands together, his harsh features and light scars dotting his face and hands, his red hair showing tinges of grey. His shifting smile belies a youthful vigor as you approach with the trophies. "Aldor! Welcome back. How goes that bleeding mess-headed beast? I see you seem to have dropped it before you." Aldor takes the first head that he's carrying and chucks it to the ground. As it rolls across with two large thuds, the rest of the congealed liquid seeping into the wood. "Aye, that's true, but we bring a bit of an obstruction in the way." Glances to the rest of you. "Obstruction, you say. Okay. Well, this is a curious phrase. Please explain to me what has occurred in your outing on this contract." Aldor scratches the side of his face. "Well, it seems that we have a group of skags."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, let's deal with the naughty bits later.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: It takes about half an hour or so to reap the bits and pieces, then again making your way back, after you've pushed the remainder of the body into the pool itself, letting it slowly sink to the bottom. You all make your way back the path you came in an awkward, quiet march.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh, you guys set that bait out. You guys killed that ape, didn't you?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Aldor gives you a look. "Good to know we aren't the only trackers in this town."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Came back a few moments later. "She would like to see the skags now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's a high standard.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Careful where you're walking, little man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All I say is I appreciate-- yes, your bear is quite fierce."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's got giant balls, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He reaches past and looks to the wall and you see there are two dire bear heads affixed to the wall of various ancient degrees.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Don't look, Trinket. Don't look.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well. I can see that you may have breached the contract here, and there may be ways of perhaps rectifying this, should you take the pledge. The pledge itself is a process, one that I myself cannot specifically judge upon. This would have to go to my boss, the Headmaster. My wife." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I feel you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Respect.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I will see if she is available. One moment." And the halfling patters off down a hallway.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is fucking awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That would be amazing.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (laughs) "A fine trophy. Do not show him you still have that. I don't think it would go over too well. Now. Most applicants for our humble family make it maybe a month or two before the Vesper Timberlands take them. Those that remain, well. They prove worthy of our business contracts. You are all on trial by deed, under my claim and authority. Should you agree and fulfill my request, indeed, you will be absolved of your infractions against the Slayer's Take and be given membership to our age-old tradition. With membership comes with access to our lodge, food, room and board, and training facilities. Simple arms can also be provided. Those that bear our insignia may also get a discount of supplies at most of Vasselheim's merchants. Most importantly, though, you will then have access to our bounty contracts. To specify, we don't just hunt beasts and creatures. A large part of what we do is harvest from our marks' requested components. Blood, eyes, teeth, organs. We receive orders from all across Exandria, from as far as even the city Emon, which you apparently hail from."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We didn't know we were interfering with it at the time. We thought we were just doing good, and we're here to make right by that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're tourists, basically.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Which is also why you are not at the hall yet. He also said you showed interest in joining our ranks to avoid this prosecution, which I think is a very smart move." And she walks behind her desk and sits down. "Should you be cut out for our work, that is. You have proven to have slain this hydra with the accompaniment of one of our roving bands of monster hunters."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They didn't do much, but yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're quite capable fighters. Grog here almost won in his fight the other night. Sorry, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You're the one that nearly felled the Hammer."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So you have heard of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've heard of this one."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Show her the lip.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I kept a keepsake.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, perhaps this emperor and many of these scholars or seers or mages you've encountered have ordered from us specifically required components, for you see, the most powerful of magics within nations require a focus or sacrifice of some kind. Many of these require pieces of these creatures. So many contracts are from very high-paying clients that have better things to do with their time than wander skulking about the forest, seeking such things as a hydra. There's a lot of money to be gained should you be up for it. I would ask you to take a contract, complete it, and return. That is all. You would have three days to do so. Do you accept?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have a keep there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How nice for you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Greyskull.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I will make a note of it." And she reaches over and grabs a quill and not even looking at the paper, just scribbles.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're friends with the emperor, thank you.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Your trial is to take the contract and you have three days to complete it."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What is involved in signing or binding this contract?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is a small ritual that binds you to the contract. Within this time it must be completed. Should you fail, the contract is absolved, and your membership is revoked."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's a trial just to bind the contract?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Precisely. Also a part of the three-day contract, with three days before it is null and void."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What is involved in signing or binding this contract?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There is a small ritual that binds you to the contract. Within this time it must be completed. Should you fail, the contract is absolved, and your membership is revoked."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's a trial just to bind the contract?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Your trial is to take the contract and you have three days to complete it."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is there a payout on this contract?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh yes, you will still receive the money for it, and with membership, from what I have been told by my husband, possibly even share in the bounty for this hydra you helped slay earlier on this day."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's good, because otherwise they didn't get any credit, right? Because it was like, magically--

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Gnomes... Three days. Will you accept?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Precisely. Also a part of the three-day contract, with three days before it is null and void."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've taken out a beholder and a handful of illithid. We're not really worried about your contract. Give us your contract; we'll sign it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She chuckles under her breath. "That is good to know," she says disbelievingly.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's an insignia that we get? Is it given to us on some sort of jacket for members only? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She reaches to part of her shoulder pauldron, lifts it up, and you can see there's a brand on the side of her arm.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: My body's a temple. I don't like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Then perhaps you'd like the judgment."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The judgment? Oh no, no. Do I have to get branded?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It does not hurt much."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whimpers) I'm really wimpy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "You will be taking your contract with a couple of other hopeful possible members, as well. Three days to fulfill. Now, cutthroat. Dragonborn. Vine-speaker. You will form the other team."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: My body's a temple. I don't like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Then perhaps you'd like the judgment."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: The judgment? Oh no, no. Do I have to get branded?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It does not hurt much."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whimpers) I'm really wimpy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Gnomes... Three days. Will you accept?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's do it. We're totally tough.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Could you do mine in henna? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I will take that as an acceptance on your part, as well."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. Yes. I'm with my group.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good, good. Now, I trust you work well as a team, but I am curious how well you work with others. As this is a test, I will be dividing your band between two contracts."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ex-squeeze me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Two teams."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we get to pick the teams?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You, gnome. Giant. Ranger. Tinkerer. You will form the first team."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is bullshit.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Your bear is allowed, yes. I shall now look over our extending contracts to decide your test for tomorrow. Go. Return in the morning fed, armed, and ready to set out. And hopefully get affiliated with your journeymen members. Now, be gone. I have business to do."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It does not hurt much."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (whimpers) I'm really wimpy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Gnomes... Three days. Will you accept?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's do it. We're totally tough.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Could you do mine in henna? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I will take that as an acceptance on your part, as well."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. Yes. I'm with my group.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good, good. Now, I trust you work well as a team, but I am curious how well you work with others. As this is a test, I will be dividing your band between two contracts."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ex-squeeze me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Two teams."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we get to pick the teams?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You, gnome. Giant. Ranger. Tinkerer. You will form the first team."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is bullshit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You will be taking your contract with a couple of other hopeful possible members, as well. Three days to fulfill. Now, cutthroat. Dragonborn. Vine-speaker. You will form the other team."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does my bear get to come with me?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "In the morning  fed, armed, and ready." Knocks the desk twice. Her husband closes the door behind you. You take a deep breath and realize this will be a very interesting challenge.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, no. We work as a pair. Maybe you don't know that about us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, then this should prove a very interesting test."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You don't understand, we haven't been apart in about--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ever.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Life is hard here in Vasselheim. Deal with disappointment."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm sorry. What was your name again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I am known as the Huntmaster around these parts. But should you wish the name in its full, Huntmaster Vanessa Cyndrial."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. Vanessa... Shitmaster...

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't. I grab him and I start walking out of the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Vanessa?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Cyndrial. C-Y-N-D-R-I-A-L."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I am not going to spell that right.
